title: cornus racemosa
intro: Cornus racemosa, also known as the gray dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Cornus racemosa include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Cornus racemosa is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, to treat a variety of health issues and promote overall well-being.
